title: Stop Corrupt Iranian Oligarchs and Entities Act
short_title: Stop Corrupt Iranian Oligarchs and Entities Act
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Financial Services, for a period to be
plain_summary: This law tells the Treasury Department to write a report about rich and powerful people in Iran who work closely with the Iranian government. The report must also include information about big companies that are partly owned by Iran's government, especially ones that made at least 2 billion dollars in 2016 and are at least 25 percent owned by the government. The Treasury must find out how these people make their money, what businesses they own in other countries, and what would happen if America put sanctions or other punishments on them. The report must be finished within 180 days and given to Congress.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Financial Services, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop Corrupt Iranian Oligarchs and Entities Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Report on Oligarchs and Parastatal Entities of Iran
sections.text: Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Secretary of the Treasury must work with the Director of National Intelligence and the Secretary of State to create a detailed report. They must send this report to the correct committees in Congress. The report must include information about senior foreign political figures and oligarchs in Iran. Senior foreign political figures are important government officials or people connected to the government. Oligarchs are very wealthy people who have a lot of power and influence. The report must identify the most important senior foreign political figures and oligarchs in Iran based on how close they are to the Iranian Government. It must also estimate how much money each person is worth. The report must explain the relationship between these people and President Masoud Pezeshkian or other members of the Iranian ruling elite. It must identify any signs of corruption involving these people. The report must list known sources of income for these people and their family members including spouses, children, parents, and siblings. This includes information about who really owns businesses that these people might control. It must identify any business connections these people have outside of Iran. The report must also include information about Iranian parastatal entities. Parastatal entities are companies that are partly owned by the government. The report must explain how these Iranian parastatal entities started and what role they play in Iran's economy. It must describe how these entities are led and who really owns them. It must identify any business connections these entities have outside of Iran. The report must include information about how much key economic sectors in the United States are exposed to Iranian politically connected people, Iranian parastatal entities, and Iranian State-owned enterprises. These sectors include at least banking, securities, insurance, and real estate. The report must include information about what would likely happen if debt and equity restrictions were put on Iranian parastatal entities. It must also explain what would happen if Iranian parastatal entities were added to the list of Specially Designated Nationals. This is a list maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control at the Department of the Treasury of people and organizations that Americans are not allowed to do business with. The report must include information about what could happen if sanctions or debt and equity restrictions were put on Iranian oligarchs, Iranian parastatal entities, or Iranian State-owned enterprises in addition to any that already exist. This includes impacts on the oligarchs, entities, and enterprises themselves and on Iran's economy. It also includes impacts on the economies of the United States and United States allies. The report must be submitted in an unclassified form but may contain a classified annex. An unclassified form means most people can read it. A classified annex is a secret section that only certain people with security clearance can read. In this section, appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Financial Services, and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, the Committee on Foreign Relations, and the Committee on Finance of the Senate. Iranian parastatal entities means entities where Iranian State ownership is at least twenty five percent and that had two thousand sixteen revenues of approximately two billion dollars or more. Senior foreign political figure has the same meaning as given in section one thousand ten point six zero five of title thirty one, Code of Federal Regulations or any similar regulation or ruling.
